category_title: Students who exhibit task avoidance through vocalizations and floor-dropping behaviors
minimal_context: The student and instructor are walking down the hallway to practice travel skills using a long cane.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to practice hallway travel with your long cane. Let's start walking.

STUDENT: (Walks halfway down the hall while being partially held and physically prompted)

TUTOR: Now try walking by yourself.

STUDENT: (Agrees to walk independently and takes a few steps without being held)

TUTOR: Keep going down the hall.

STUDENT: (Drops to the ground again, begins screaming and crying, and refuses to stand or move)

options.A: Tell the student, "Don't say you can't, you can," to encourage them to stand up and continue walking.
options.B: Stop singing as soon as the student cries or drops to the ground, and maintain singing only during successful hallway travel.
options.C: Kneel in front of the student and say, "Hands out, hold the cane... 1, 2, 3, hold the cane, walk."
options.D: Provide verbal information to the student regarding the specific nature of the breakdown in their hallway travel.
